steps.title: Tie vulnerability to preference
steps.content: Konno and colleagues suggest iris color made some dogs seem more vulnerable and in need of protection, which may explain participants' willingness to interact with or keep those dogs. The data must connect vulnerability-related ratings to preference.

steps.title: Use the matching table pattern
steps.content: Choice A links maturity and iris color but not preference. Choice C compares maturity ratings without willingness to interact or keep. Choice D switches the mechanism to friendliness. The needed finding says favored dogs were rated less mature than the comparison dogs. The answer is B.
